operating_state: Net sales increased 32% to $12.7 billion driven by higher shipment volumes across all segments and price realization in most end markets. Production and precision agriculture posted significant growth from increased North American and Latin American volumes, while small agriculture, turf, construction and forestry segments achieved double-digit growth. Operating margins expanded significantly across all segments due to improved supply chain conditions enabling higher production levels and price realization offsetting elevated production costs.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio improved to 69.6% from 78.5% primarily due to price realization partially offset by higher production costs from elevated component, energy, salary, and wage expenses. Research and development expenses increased 23% reflecting continued technology investment focus. Selling, administrative and general expenses rose 22% mainly from higher employee compensation driven by inflationary conditions and profit-sharing incentives.
material_events: Company repurchased $1.3 billion of common stock during the quarter compared to $623 million in prior year. Board declared quarterly dividend of $1.25 per share increased from $1.05 per share. Russian financial services business met held-for-sale criteria with completion expected in Q2 2023, reversing credit loss allowances and recording valuation allowances. Financial services entered retail note securitization transactions totaling $1.3 billion in secured borrowings.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments generated $681 million positive impact compared to $267 million negative impact in prior year from strengthening international currencies. Rising interest rates compressed financial services spreads by $53 million after-tax with spread compression expected to persist through 2023. Supply chain disruptions continued into 2023 though showing improvement during Q1 with reduced past due deliveries from suppliers.
forward_outlook: Management expects to sell more large agricultural equipment in 2023 than 2022 across major markets with order books full through majority of 2023. Dealer inventory replenishment expected in 2024. Financial services net income anticipated lower than fiscal 2022 due to less favorable financing spreads, higher expenses, and lower operating lease disposition gains.
uncertainty_profile: Supply chain disruptions expected to persist into 2023 potentially impacting customer demand fulfillment. Technology investment monetization and marketplace adoption holds elevated uncertainty levels. Global political conditions, trade policies, and commodity price volatility remain operational concerns.
